action.reasoning: Observation: The current best test balanced_accuracy is 0.8539 from iteration 16 (ensemble_fine.pt), achieved with flip-TTA predict.py. Iteration 20 switched predict.py to multi-scale TTA and the score dropped to 0.8515, suggesting multi-scale TTA hurts on this small-image dataset. Analysis: I need to (1) revert predict.py to flip-only TTA to match the best result's inference pipeline, and (2) train a diverse third model to improve the ensemble. The best single-model recipe (iter9) uses natural-sampled CE with ConvNeXt-Tiny. To create a diverse ensemble member, I will train another ConvNeXt-Tiny from scratch using Focal Loss (gamma=1.0), which down-weights easy head-class examples and focuses on hard tail-class examples, learning different representations. Then I'll ensemble it with best_iter9.pt via TTA-aware grid search. Decision: Rewrite predict.py with flip-only TTA, rewrite train.py to support Focal Loss, rewrite model.py for robustness, then train a focal-loss model (seed=456), run ensemble tuning, smoke-test, and submit eval.
action.command: python train.py --loss focal --gamma 1.0 --seed 456 --epochs 20 --output checkpoints/focal.pt && python tune_ensemble_fine.py --ckpt1 checkpoints/best_iter9.pt --ckpt2 checkpoints/focal.pt --output checkpoints/ensemble_focal.pt && mkdir -p fake_test && cp /data/val.pt fake_test/test.pt && python predict.py --data_path fake_test --checkpoint checkpoints/ensemble_focal.pt --output fake_out.json
